NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To the exiles of the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
text.Rotherham: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, unto the chosen pilgrims of the dispersion, throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia and Bithynia,—
text.KJV: 1:1 Peter an Apostle of Iesus Christ, to the strangers scattred thorowout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Address Chapter 1 - Greeting. Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen sojourners of the dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

text.NRSV: who have been chosen and destined by God the Father and sanctified by the Spirit to be obedient to Jesus Christ and to be sprinkled with his blood: May grace and peace be yours in abundance.
text.Rotherham: [Chosen] according to the fore-knowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of Spirit, unto obedience and the sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ, Favour unto you, and peace, be multiplied!
text.KJV: 1:2 Elect, according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit vnto obedience, and sprinkling of the blood of Iesus Christ: Grace vnto you and peace be multiplied.
text.NABRE: 1:2 in the foreknowledge of God the Father, through sanctification by the Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling with the blood of Jesus Christ: may grace and peace be yours in abundance.

text.NRSV: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! By his great mercy he has given us a new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
text.Rotherham: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, Who, according to his great mercy, hath regenerated us unto a living hope, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from among the dead,
text.KJV: 1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Iesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy, hath begotten vs againe vnto a liuely hope, by the resurrection of Iesus Christ from the dead,
text.NABRE: 1:3 II. The Gift and Call of God in Baptism - Blessing. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who in his great mercy gave us a new birth to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,

text.NRSV: and into an inheritance that is imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven for you,
text.Rotherham: Unto an inheritance, incorruptible and undefiled and unfading, reserved in the heavens for you
text.KJV: 1:4 To an inheritance incorruptible, and vndefiled, and that fadeth not away, reserued in heauen for you,
text.NABRE: 1:4 to an inheritance that is imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven for you

text.NRSV: who are being protected by the power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
text.Rotherham: who, in God’s power, are being guarded through faith unto salvation—ready to be revealed in the last ripe time:
text.KJV: 1:5 Who are kept by the power of God through faith vnto saluation, ready to be reuealed in the last time.
text.NABRE: 1:5 who by the power of God are safeguarded through faith, to a salvation that is ready to be revealed in the final time.

text.NRSV: In this you rejoice, even if now for a little while you have had to suffer various trials,
text.Rotherham: Wherein ye exult, though, for a little, just now, if needful, put to grief in manifold temptations,
text.KJV: 1:6 Wherin ye greatly reioyce, though now for a season (if neede bee) yee are in heauinesse through manifolde temptations:
text.NABRE: 1:6 In this you rejoice, although now for a little while you may have to suffer through various trials,

text.NRSV: so that the genuineness of your faith—being more precious than gold that, though perishable, is tested by fire—may be found to result in praise and glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
text.Rotherham: In order that the proving of your faith—much more precious than of gold that perisheth even though, by means of fire, it is proved—may be found unto praise and glory and honour in the revealing of Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: 1:7 That the triall of your faith, being much more precious then of golde that perisheth, though it bee tryed with fire, might be found vnto praise, and honor, and glory, at the appearing of Iesus Christ:
text.NABRE: 1:7 so that the genuineness of your faith, more precious than gold that is perishable even though tested by fire, may prove to be for praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Although you have not seen him, you love him; and even though you do not see him now, you believe in him and rejoice with an indescribable and glorious joy,
text.Rotherham: Whom, not having seen, ye love, on whom, though at present not looking, but believing, ye exult with joy unspeakable and filled with glory,
text.KJV: 1:8 Whom hauing not seene, yee loue, in whom though now ye see him not, yet beleeuing, ye reioyce with ioy vnspeakeable, and full of glory,
text.NABRE: 1:8 Although you have not seen him you love him; even though you do not see him now yet believe in him, you rejoice with an indescribable and glorious joy,

text.NRSV: for you are receiving the outcome of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
text.Rotherham: Being about to bear away the end of your faith—a salvation of souls:
text.KJV: 1:9 Receiuing the ende of your faith, euen the saluation of your soules:
text.NABRE: 1:9 as you attain the goal of [your] faith, the salvation of your souls.

text.NRSV: Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied of the grace that was to be yours made careful search and inquiry,
text.Rotherham: Concerning which salvation, prophets—who concerning the favour for you, did prophesy—sought out and searched out,
text.KJV: 1:10 Of which saluation the Prophets haue inquired, and searched diligently, who prophesied of the grace that should come vnto you,
text.NABRE: 1:10 Concerning this salvation, prophets who prophesied about the grace that was to be yours searched and investigated it,

text.NRSV: inquiring about the person or time that the Spirit of Christ within them indicated when it testified in advance to the sufferings destined for Christ and the subsequent glory.
text.Rotherham: Searching into what particular, or what manner, of season the Spirit of Christ which was in them was pointing to, when witnessing beforehand as to—The sufferings, for Christ, and the glories, after these,—
text.KJV: 1:11 Searching what, or what maner of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them did signifie, when it testified beforehand the suffrings of Christ, and the glory that should follow.
text.NABRE: 1:11 investigating the time and circumstances that the Spirit of Christ within them indicated when it testified in advance to the sufferings destined for Christ and the glories to follow them.

text.NRSV: It was revealed to them that they were serving not themselves but you, in regard to the things that have now been announced to you through those who brought you good news by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven—things into which angels long to look.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom it was revealed—that, Not unto themselves, but unto us, they were ministering them, which things have, now, been announced unto you through them who have told you the good tidings with Holy Spirit sent forth from heaven: into which things messengers are coveting to obtain a nearer view.
text.KJV: 1:12 Unto whome it was reuealed, that not vnto themselues, but vnto vs, they did minister the things which are now reported vnto you, by them that haue preached the Gospel vnto you, with the holy Ghost sent downe from heauen, which things the Angels desire to looke into.
text.NABRE: 1:12 It was revealed to them that they were serving not themselves but you with regard to the things that have now been announced to you by those who preached the good news to you [through] the holy Spirit sent from heaven, things into which angels longed to look.

text.NRSV: Therefore prepare your minds for action; discipline yourselves; set all your hope on the grace that Jesus Christ will bring you when he is revealed.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, girding up the loins of your mind, keeping sober, perseveringly direct your hope unto the favour, being borne along to you, in the revealing of Jesus Christ:
text.KJV: 1:13 Wherefore gird vp the loynes of your minde, bee sober, and hope to the end, for the grace that is to bee brought vnto you at the reuelation of Iesus Christ:
text.NABRE: 1:13 Obedience. Therefore, gird up the loins of your mind, live soberly, and set your hopes completely on the grace to be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Like obedient children, do not be conformed to the desires that you formerly had in ignorance.
text.Rotherham: As obedient persons, not configuring yourselves unto your former covetings in your ignorance:
text.KJV: 1:14 As obedient children, not fashioning your selues according to the former lusts, in your ignorance:
text.NABRE: 1:14 Like obedient children, do not act in compliance with the desires of your former ignorance

text.NRSV: Instead, as he who called you is holy, be holy yourselves in all your conduct;
text.Rotherham: But, according as he that hath called you is holy, do, ye yourselves, also become, holy in all manner of behaviour,—
text.KJV: 1:15 But as hee which hath called you is holy, so be ye holy in all maner of conuersation;
text.NABRE: 1:15 but, as he who called you is holy, be holy yourselves in every aspect of your conduct,

text.NRSV: for it is written, “You shall be holy, for I am holy.”
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as it is written—Holy shall ye be, because, I, am holy.
text.KJV: 1:16 Because it is written, Be ye holy, for I am holy.
text.NABRE: 1:16 for it is written, “Be holy because I [am] holy.”

text.NRSV: If you invoke as Father the one who judges all people impartially according to their deeds, live in reverent fear during the time of your exile.
text.Rotherham: And, if, as Father, ye are invoking him who, without respect of persons, judgeth according to each man’s work, with reverence, for the time of your sojourning, behave ye;
text.KJV: 1:17 And if ye call on the Father, who without respect of persons iudgeth according to euery mans worke, passe the time of your soiourning here in feare:
text.NABRE: 1:17 Reverence. Now if you invoke as Father him who judges impartially according to each one’s works, conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your sojourning,

text.NRSV: You know that you were ransomed from the futile ways inherited from your ancestors, not with perishable things like silver or gold,
text.Rotherham: Knowing that, Not with corruptible things, with silver or gold, have ye been redeemed from your unmeaning behaviour paternally handed down,
text.KJV: 1:18 For as much as ye know that yee were not redeemed with corruptible things, as siluer and golde, from your vaine conuersation receiued by tradition from your fathers;
text.NABRE: 1:18 realizing that you were ransomed from your futile conduct, handed on by your ancestors, not with perishable things like silver or gold

text.NRSV: but with the precious blood of Christ, like that of a lamb without defect or blemish.
text.Rotherham: But with precious blood, as of a lamb, unblemished and unspotted, of an Anointed One,—
text.KJV: 1:19 But with the precious blood of Christ, as of a Lambe without blemish and without spot,
text.NABRE: 1:19 but with the precious blood of Christ as of a spotless unblemished lamb.

text.NRSV: He was destined before the foundation of the world, but was revealed at the end of the ages for your sake.
text.Rotherham: Foreknown, indeed, before the foundation of the world, but made manifest at a last stage of the times, for the sake of you
text.KJV: 1:20 Who verily was foreordeined before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you.
text.NABRE: 1:20 He was known before the foundation of the world but revealed in the final time for you,

text.NRSV: Through him you have come to trust in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are set on God.
text.Rotherham: who, through him, are faithful towards God,—Who raised him from among the dead, and glory to him gave, So that your faith and hope are directed towards God:
text.KJV: 1:21 Who by him do beleeue in God that raised him vp from the dead, and gaue him glorie, that your faith and hope might be in God.
text.NABRE: 1:21 who through him believe in God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God. Mutual Love.

text.NRSV: Now that you have purified your souls by your obedience to the truth so that you have genuine mutual love, love one another deeply from the heart.
text.Rotherham: Having purified, your souls, by the obedience of the truth, unto unfeigned brotherly affection, from the heart, love, one another, earnestly;
text.KJV: 1:22 Seeing yee haue purified your soules in obeying the truth through the Spirit, vnto vnfained loue of the brethren: see that ye loue one another with a pure heart feruently,
text.NABRE: 1:22 Since you have purified yourselves by obedience to the truth for sincere mutual love, love one another intensely from a [pure] heart.

text.NRSV: You have been born anew, not of perishable but of imperishable seed, through the living and enduring word of God.
text.Rotherham: Having been regenerated—Not out of corruptible seed, but incorruptible—through means of the word of a Living and Abiding God;
text.KJV: 1:23 Being borne againe, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God which liueth and abideth for euer.
text.NABRE: 1:23 You have been born anew, not from perishable but from imperishable seed, through the living and abiding word of God,

text.NRSV: For “All flesh is like grass and all its glory like the flower of grass. The grass withers, and the flower falls,
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as—All flesh, is as grass, and, all the glory thereof, as the flower of grass,—The grass hath withered, and the flower hath fallen out,
text.KJV: 1:24 For all flesh is as grasse, and all the glory of man as the flowre of grasse: the grasse withereth, and the flowre thereof falleth away.
text.NABRE: 1:24 for: “All flesh is like grass, and all its glory like the flower of the field; the grass withers, and the flower wilts;

text.NRSV: but the word of the Lord endures forever.” That word is the good news that was announced to you.
text.Rotherham: But the declaration of the Lord age-abidingly remaineth; And, this, is a declaration which in the joyful message hath been announced unto you.
text.KJV: 1:25 But the word of the Lord endureth for euer: & this is the word which by the Gospel is preached vnto you.
text.NABRE: 1:25 but the word of the Lord remains forever.” This is the word that has been proclaimed to you.

text.NRSV: Rid yourselves, therefore, of all malice, and all guile, insincerity, envy, and all slander.
text.Rotherham: Putting away, therefore, all vice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envyings and all detractions,
text.KJV: 2:1 Wherefore laying aside all malice, and all guile, and hypocrisies, and enuies, and euill speakings,
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 - God’s House and People. Rid yourselves of all malice and all deceit, insincerity, envy, and all slander;

text.NRSV: Like newborn infants, long for the pure, spiritual milk, so that by it you may grow into salvation—
text.Rotherham: As new-born babes, for the pure milk that is for the mind, eagerly crave, that, thereby, ye may grow unto salvation:—
text.KJV: 2:2 As new borne babes desire the sincere milke of the word, that ye may grow thereby,
text.NABRE: 2:2 like newborn infants, long for pure spiritual milk so that through it you may grow into salvation,

text.NRSV: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is good.
text.Rotherham: If ye have tasted for yourselves that, gracious, is the Lord,—
text.KJV: 2:3 If so bee yee haue tasted that the Lord is gracious.
text.NABRE: 2:3 for you have tasted that the Lord is good.

text.NRSV: Come to him, a living stone, though rejected by mortals yet chosen and precious in God’s sight, and
text.Rotherham: Unto whom coming near, as unto a living stone—by men, indeed, rejected, but, with God, chosen, held in honour,
text.KJV: 2:4 To whom comming as vnto a liuing Stone, disallowed in deed of men, but chosen of God, and precious,
text.NABRE: 2:4 Come to him, a living stone, rejected by human beings but chosen and precious in the sight of God,

text.NRSV: like living stones, let yourselves be built into a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Yourselves also, as living stones, are being built up a spiritual house, for a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices, well-pleasing unto God through Jesus Christ;
text.KJV: 2:5 Ye also as liuely stones, are built vp a spirituall house, an holy Priesthood to offer vp spirituall sacrifice, acceptable to God by Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 2:5 and, like living stones, let yourselves be built into a spiritual house to be a holy priesthood to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: For it stands in scripture: “See, I am laying in Zion a stone, a cornerstone chosen and precious; and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.”
text.Rotherham: Inasmuch as it is contained in scripture—Lo! I lay in Zion an outmost corner stone, chosen, held in honour, and, he that resteth faith thereupon, shall not be put to shame.
text.KJV: 2:6 Wherefore it is conteined in the Scripture, Beholde, I lay in Sion a chiefe corner stone, elect, precious, and he that beleeueth on him, shall not be confounded.
text.NABRE: 2:6 For it says in scripture: “Behold, I am laying a stone in Zion, a cornerstone, chosen and precious, and whoever believes in it shall not be put to shame.”

text.NRSV: To you then who believe, he is precious; but for those who do not believe, “The stone that the builders rejected has become the very head of the corner,”
text.Rotherham: Unto you, then, is the honour—[unto you] who believe; but, unto such as disbelieve, The stone which the builders rejected,—The same, hath become head of the corner,
text.KJV: 2:7 Unto you therfore which beleeue hee is precious; but vnto them which be disobedient, the stone which the builders disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner,
text.NABRE: 2:7 Therefore, its value is for you who have faith, but for those without faith: “The stone which the builders rejected has become the cornerstone,”

text.NRSV: and “A stone that makes them stumble, and a rock that makes them fall.” They stumble because they disobey the word, as they were destined to do.
text.Rotherham: And a stone to strike against, and a rock to stumble over: Who stumble because, unto the word, they do not yield,—unto which also they have been appointed;
text.KJV: 2:8 And a Stone of stumbling, and a Rocke of offence, euen to them which stumble at the word, being disobedient, whereunto also they were appointed.
text.NABRE: 2:8 and “A stone that will make people stumble, and a rock that will make them fall.” They stumble by disobeying the word, as is their destiny.

text.NRSV: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s own people, in order that you may proclaim the mighty acts of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for a peculiar treasure, that, the excellences, ye may tell forth, of him who, out of darkness, hath called you into his marvellous light:—
text.KJV: 2:9 But yee are a chosen generation, a royall Priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people, that yee should shewe forth the praises of him, who hath called you out of darknes into his marueilous light:
text.NABRE: 2:9 But you are “a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people of his own, so that you may announce the praises” of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.

text.NRSV: Once you were not a people, but now you are God’s people; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
text.Rotherham: Who, at one time, were a No-people, but, now, are a people of God,—who had not been enjoying mercy, but, now, have received mercy.
text.KJV: 2:10 Which in time past were not a people, but are now the people of God: which had not obteined mercie, but now haue obteined mercy.
text.NABRE: 2:10 Once you were “no people” but now you are God’s people; you “had not received mercy” but now you have received mercy.

text.NRSV: Beloved, I urge you as aliens and exiles to abstain from the desires of the flesh that wage war against the soul.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! I exhort you, as sojourners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly covetings, such as take the field against the soul;
text.KJV: 2:11 Dearely beloued, I beseech you as strangers and pilgrimes, abstaine from fleshly lusts, which warre against the soule,
text.NABRE: 2:11 III. The Christian in a Hostile World - Christian Examples. Beloved, I urge you as aliens and sojourners to keep away from worldly desires that wage war against the soul.

text.NRSV: Conduct yourselves honorably among the Gentiles, so that, though they malign you as evildoers, they may see your good deeds and glorify God on the day of visitation.
text.Rotherham: Having, your behaviour among the nations, honourable,—in order that, wherein they speak against you as evil-doers, they may, owing to the honourable works they are permitted to behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.
text.KJV: 2:12 Hauing your conuersation honest among the Gentiles, that whereas they speake against you as euill doers, they may by your good works which they shall behold, glorifie God in the day of visitation.
text.NABRE: 2:12 Maintain good conduct among the Gentiles, so that if they speak of you as evildoers, they may observe your good works and glorify God on the day of visitation. Christian Citizens.

text.NRSV: For the Lord’s sake accept the authority of every human institution, whether of the emperor as supreme,
text.Rotherham: Submit yourselves unto every human creation, for the Lord’s sake,—whether unto a king, as one that protecteth,
text.KJV: 2:13 Submit your selues to euery ordinance of man for the Lordes sake, whether it be to the King, as supreme,
text.NABRE: 2:13 Be subject to every human institution for the Lord’s sake, whether it be to the king as supreme

text.NRSV: or of governors, as sent by him to punish those who do wrong and to praise those who do right.
text.Rotherham: Or unto governors, as, through him, sent—for vengeance on evil-doers, but praise to such as do good;
text.KJV: 2:14 Or vnto gouernours, as vnto them that are sent by him, for the punishment of euil doers, and for the praise of them that doe well.
text.NABRE: 2:14 or to governors as sent by him for the punishment of evildoers and the approval of those who do good.

text.NRSV: For it is God’s will that by doing right you should silence the ignorance of the foolish.
text.Rotherham: Because, so, is the will of God,—by doing good, to be putting to silence, the ignorance of heedless men:
text.KJV: 2:15 For so is the will of God, that with well doing yee may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men.
text.NABRE: 2:15 For it is the will of God that by doing good you may silence the ignorance of foolish people.

text.NRSV: As servants of God, live as free people, yet do not use your freedom as a pretext for evil.
text.Rotherham: As free, yet, not as a cloak of vice, holding your freedom, but, as God’s servants.
text.KJV: 2:16 As free, and not vsing your libertie for a cloake of maliciousnesse, but as the seruants of God.
text.NABRE: 2:16 Be free, yet without using freedom as a pretext for evil, but as slaves of God.

text.NRSV: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Fear God. Honor the emperor.
text.Rotherham: Unto all men, give honour, unto the brotherhood, shew love, unto God, give reverence, the king, hold in honour.
text.KJV: 2:17 Honour all men. Loue the brotherhood. Feare God. Honour the King.
text.NABRE: 2:17 Give honor to all, love the community, fear God, honor the king.

text.NRSV: Slaves, accept the authority of your masters with all deference, not only those who are kind and gentle but also those who are harsh.
text.Rotherham: Ye domestics, submitting yourselves, in all reverence, unto your masters,—not only unto the good and considerate, but also unto the perverse;
text.KJV: 2:18 Seruants, be subiect to your masters with al feare, not only to the good and gentle, but also to the froward.
text.NABRE: 2:18 Christian Slaves. Slaves, be subject to your masters with all reverence, not only to those who are good and equitable but also to those who are perverse.

text.NRSV: For it is a credit to you if, being aware of God, you endure pain while suffering unjustly.
text.Rotherham: For, this, is thankworthy,—If, for the sake of conscience towards God, one beareth griefs, suffering wrongfully,—
text.KJV: 2:19 For this is thanke-worthie, if a man for conscience toward God endure griefe, suffering wrongfully.
text.NABRE: 2:19 For whenever anyone bears the pain of unjust suffering because of consciousness of God, that is a grace.

text.NRSV: If you endure when you are beaten for doing wrong, what credit is that? But if you endure when you do right and suffer for it, you have God’s approval.
text.Rotherham: For what sort of honour is it—if, committing sin, and being buffeted, ye endure it? But, if, doing good, and suffering, ye endure it, this, is thankworthy with God.
text.KJV: 2:20 For what glory is it, if when yee be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it patiently: but if when yee doe well, and suffer for it, ye take it patiently, this is acceptable with God.
text.NABRE: 2:20 But what credit is there if you are patient when beaten for doing wrong? But if you are patient when you suffer for doing what is good, this is a grace before God.

text.NRSV: For to this you have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, so that you should follow in his steps.
text.Rotherham: For, hereunto, have ye been called; because, Christ also, suffered in your behalf, unto you, leaving behind a pattern, that ye might follow in his steps:
text.KJV: 2:21 For euen hereunto were ye called: because Christ also suffered for vs, leauing vs an example, that yee should follow his steps.
text.NABRE: 2:21 For to this you have been called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example that you should follow in his footsteps.

text.NRSV: “He committed no sin, and no deceit was found in his mouth.”
text.Rotherham: Who did not, a sin, commit, neither was deceit found in his mouth,—
text.KJV: 2:22 Who did no sinne, neither was guile found in his mouth.
text.NABRE: 2:22 “He committed no sin, and no deceit was found in his mouth.”

text.NRSV: When he was abused, he did not return abuse; when he suffered, he did not threaten; but he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
text.Rotherham: Who, being reviled, was not reviling again, suffering, he was not threatening, but was making surrender unto him that judgeth righteously,—
text.KJV: 2:23 Who when hee was reuiled, reuiled not againe; when hee suffered, hee threatned not, but committed himselfe to him that iudgeth righteously.
text.NABRE: 2:23 When he was insulted, he returned no insult; when he suffered, he did not threaten; instead, he handed himself over to the one who judges justly.

text.NRSV: He himself bore our sins in his body on the cross, so that, free from sins, we might live for righteousness; by his wounds you have been healed.
text.Rotherham: Who, our sins, himself, bare up, in his body, unto the tree, in order that we, from our sins getting away, in righteousness, might live,—by whose stripes, ye have been healed;
text.KJV: 2:24 Who his owne selfe bare our sinnes in his owne body on the tree, that wee being dead to sinnes, should liue vnto righteousnesse, by whose stripes ye were healed.
text.NABRE: 2:24 He himself bore our sins in his body upon the cross, so that, free from sin, we might live for righteousness. By his wounds you have been healed.

text.NRSV: For you were going astray like sheep, but now you have returned to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.
text.Rotherham: For ye were as sheep going astray, but have now turned back unto the shepherd and overseer of your souls.
text.KJV: 2:25 For yee were as sheepe going astray, but are now returned vnto the shepheard and Bishop of your soules.
text.NABRE: 2:25 For you had gone astray like sheep, but you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.

text.NRSV: Wives, in the same way, accept the authority of your husbands, so that, even if some of them do not obey the word, they may be won over without a word by their wives’ conduct,
text.Rotherham: In like manner, ye wives,—submitting yourselves unto your own husbands; in order that, if any are not yielding unto the word, through their wives behaviour, they may, without the word, be won,
text.KJV: 3:1 Likewise, ye wiues, be in subiection to your owne husbands, that if any obey not the word, they also may without the word be wonne by the conuersation of the wiues:
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 - Christian Spouses. Likewise, you wives should be subordinate to your husbands so that, even if some disobey the word, they may be won over without a word by their wives’ conduct

text.NRSV: when they see the purity and reverence of your lives.
text.Rotherham: Having been permitted to behold your reverently chaste behaviour,—
text.KJV: 3:2 While they beholde your chaste conuersation coupled with feare:
text.NABRE: 3:2 when they observe your reverent and chaste behavior.

text.NRSV: Do not adorn yourselves outwardly by braiding your hair, and by wearing gold ornaments or fine clothing;
text.Rotherham: Whose adorning, let it be—not the outward, of plaiting the hair and wearing golden ornaments, or putting on of apparel,
text.KJV: 3:3 Whose adorning, let it not bee that outward adorning, of plaiting the haire, and of wearing of gold, or of putting on of apparell.
text.NABRE: 3:3 Your adornment should not be an external one: braiding the hair, wearing gold jewelry, or dressing in fine clothes,

text.NRSV: rather, let your adornment be the inner self with the lasting beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in God’s sight.
text.Rotherham: But the hidden character, of the heart,—in the incorruptible [ornament] of the quiet, and meek, spirit, which is, in presence of God, of great price.
text.KJV: 3:4 But let it bee the hidden man of the heart, in that which is not corruptible, euen the ornament of a meeke and quiet spirit, which is in the sight of God of great price.
text.NABRE: 3:4 but rather the hidden character of the heart, expressed in the imperishable beauty of a gentle and calm disposition, which is precious in the sight of God.

text.NRSV: It was in this way long ago that the holy women who hoped in God used to adorn themselves by accepting the authority of their husbands.
text.Rotherham: For, so, at one time, the holy women also, who directed their hope towards God, used to adorn themselves, being in submission unto their own husbands:
text.KJV: 3:5 For after this manner in the olde time, the holy women also who trusted in God adorned themselues, beeing in subiection vnto their owne husbands.
text.NABRE: 3:5 For this is also how the holy women who hoped in God once used to adorn themselves and were subordinate to their husbands;

text.NRSV: Thus Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him lord. You have become her daughters as long as you do what is good and never let fears alarm you.
text.Rotherham: As, Sarah, was obedient unto Abraham, calling him, lord,—whose children ye have become—so long as ye are doing good, and not bringing yourselves into fear of any single cause of alarm.
text.KJV: 3:6 Euen as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him Lord, whose daughters ye are as long as ye doe well, and are not afraid with any amazement.
text.NABRE: 3:6 thus Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him “lord.” You are her children when you do what is good and fear no intimidation.

text.NRSV: Husbands, in the same way, show consideration for your wives in your life together, paying honor to the woman as the weaker sex, since they too are also heirs of the gracious gift of life—so that nothing may hinder your prayers.
text.Rotherham: Ye husbands, in like manner, dwelling with them according to knowledge,—as unto a weaker vessel, unto the female [vessel], assigning honour, as joint—inheritors also of life’s favour,—to the end that unhindered may be, your prayers.
text.KJV: 3:7 Likewise ye husbands, dwel with them according to knowledge, giuing honour vnto the wife as vnto the weaker vessel, and as being heires together of the grace of life, that your prayers be not hindered.
text.NABRE: 3:7 Likewise, you husbands should live with your wives in understanding, showing honor to the weaker female sex, since we are joint heirs of the gift of life, so that your prayers may not be hindered. Christian Conduct.

text.NRSV: Finally, all of you, have unity of spirit, sympathy, love for one another, a tender heart, and a humble mind.
text.Rotherham: And, finally, all, being of one mind, having fellow—feeling, attached to the brethren, of tender affection, of lowly mind:
text.KJV: 3:8 Finally be ye all of one minde, hauing compassion one of another, loue as brethren, be pitifull, be courteous,
text.NABRE: 3:8 Finally, all of you, be of one mind, sympathetic, loving toward one another, compassionate, humble.

text.NRSV: Do not repay evil for evil or abuse for abuse; but, on the contrary, repay with a blessing. It is for this that you were called, that you might inherit a blessing.
text.Rotherham: Not returning evil for evil, nor reviling for reviling, but, on the contrary, bestowing a blessing,—because, hereunto, have ye been called, in order that, a blessing, ye might inherit.
text.KJV: 3:9 Not rendring euill for euill, or railing for railing: but contrarywise blessing, knowing that yee are thereunto called, that ye should inherite a blessing.
text.NABRE: 3:9 Do not return evil for evil, or insult for insult; but, on the contrary, a blessing, because to this you were called, that you might inherit a blessing.

text.NRSV: For “Those who desire life and desire to see good days, let them keep their tongues from evil and their lips from speaking deceit;
text.Rotherham: For, he that desireth to love, life, and to see good days, Let him cause his tongue to cease from mischief, and lips, that they speak not deceit;
text.KJV: 3:10 For hee that will loue life, and see good dayes, let him refraine his tongue from euil, and his lips that they speake no guile:
text.NABRE: 3:10 For: “Whoever would love life and see good days must keep the tongue from evil and the lips from speaking deceit,

text.NRSV: let them turn away from evil and do good; let them seek peace and pursue it.
text.Rotherham: Let him turn away from mischief, and do good, Let him seek peace, and pursue it;
text.KJV: 3:11 Let him eschew euil and do good, let him seeke peace and ensue it.
text.NABRE: 3:11 must turn from evil and do good, seek peace and follow after it.

text.NRSV: For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears are open to their prayer. But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.”
text.Rotherham: Because, the eyes of the Lord, are towards the righteous, and his ears, unto their supplication,—Whereas, the face of the Lord, is against them that are doing mischievous things.
text.KJV: 3:12 For the eyes of the Lord are ouer the righteous, and his eares are open vnto their prayers: but the face of the Lord is against them that doe euill.
text.NABRE: 3:12 For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous and his ears turned to their prayer, but the face of the Lord is against evildoers.” Christian Suffering.

text.NRSV: Now who will harm you if you are eager to do what is good?
text.Rotherham: Who, then, is he that shall harm you, if, for that which is good, ye become zealous?
text.KJV: 3:13 And who is hee that will harme you, if ye bee followers of that which is good?
text.NABRE: 3:13 Now who is going to harm you if you are enthusiastic for what is good?

text.NRSV: But even if you do suffer for doing what is right, you are blessed. Do not fear what they fear, and do not be intimidated,
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, even if ye should suffer for righteousness’ sake, happy [are ye] ! Their fear, however, do not fear, neither be troubled;
text.KJV: 3:14 But and if ye suffer for righteousnes sake, happy are ye, and be not afraid of their terrour, neither be troubled:
text.NABRE: 3:14 But even if you should suffer because of righteousness, blessed are you. Do not be afraid or terrified with fear of them,

text.NRSV: but in your hearts sanctify Christ as Lord. Always be ready to make your defense to anyone who demands from you an accounting for the hope that is in you;
text.Rotherham: But, the Lord Christ, hallow ye in your hearts, ready always for a defence, unto every one that is asking you a reason concerning, the hope within you,—nevertheless, with meekness and reverence:
text.KJV: 3:15 But sanctifie the Lord God in your hearts, & be ready alwayes to giue an answere to euery man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you, with meekenesse and feare:
text.NABRE: 3:15 but sanctify Christ as Lord in your hearts. Always be ready to give an explanation to anyone who asks you for a reason for your hope,

text.NRSV: yet do it with gentleness and reverence. Keep your conscience clear, so that, when you are maligned, those who abuse you for your good conduct in Christ may be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: Having, a good conscience, in order that, wherein they speak against you, they may be put to shame who cast wanton insult on, your good behaviour in Christ.
text.KJV: 3:16 Hauing a good conscience, that whereas they speake euill of you, as of euill doers, they may bee ashamed that falsly accuse your good conuersation in Christ.
text.NABRE: 3:16 but do it with gentleness and reverence, keeping your conscience clear, so that, when you are maligned, those who defame your good conduct in Christ may themselves be put to shame.

text.NRSV: For it is better to suffer for doing good, if suffering should be God’s will, than to suffer for doing evil.
text.Rotherham: For it is, better, as well-doers, if it should please the pleasure of God, to be suffering, than, as evil-doers:
text.KJV: 3:17 For it is better, if the will of God be so, that yee suffer for well doing, then for euill doing.
text.NABRE: 3:17 For it is better to suffer for doing good, if that be the will of God, than for doing evil.

text.NRSV: For Christ also suffered for sins once for all, the righteous for the unrighteous, in order to bring you to God. He was put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit,
text.Rotherham: Because, Christ also, once for all, concerning sins, died,—Just in behalf of unjust,—in order that he might introduce us unto God; being put to death, indeed, in flesh, but made alive in spirit,—
text.KJV: 3:18 For Christ also hath once suffered for sinnes, the iust for the vniust, that he might bring vs to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit.
text.NABRE: 3:18 For Christ also suffered for sins once, the righteous for the sake of the unrighteous, that he might lead you to God. Put to death in the flesh, he was brought to life in the spirit.

text.NRSV: in which also he went and made a proclamation to the spirits in prison,
text.Rotherham: In which, even unto the spirits in prison, he went and proclaimed,—
text.KJV: 3:19 By which also he went and preached vnto the spirits in prison,
text.NABRE: 3:19 In it he also went to preach to the spirits in prison,

text.NRSV: who in former times did not obey, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah, during the building of the ark, in which a few, that is, eight persons, were saved through water.
text.Rotherham: [Spirits] unyielding at one time, when the longsuffering of God was holding forth a welcome in the days of Noah, there being in preparation an ark—[going] into which, a few, that is eight, souls, were brought safely through by means of water,—
text.KJV: 3:20 Which sometime were disobedient, when once the long-suffering of God waited in the dayes of Noah, while the Arke was a preparing: wherein few, that is, eight soules were saued by water.
text.NABRE: 3:20 who had once been disobedient while God patiently waited in the days of Noah during the building of the ark, in which a few persons, eight in all, were saved through water.

text.NRSV: And baptism, which this prefigured, now saves you—not as a removal of dirt from the body, but as an appeal to God for a good conscience, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: Which [water] in manner corresponding, doth, now, save, you also—even immersion,—not a putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the request unto God, for a good conscience, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: 3:21 The like figure whereunto, euen Baptisme, doth also now saue vs, (not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answere of a good conscience toward God,) by the resurrection of Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 3:21 This prefigured baptism, which saves you now. It is not a removal of dirt from the body but an appeal to God for a clear conscience, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, with angels, authorities, and powers made subject to him.
text.Rotherham: Who is on the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, messengers and authorities and powers, having been made subject unto him.
text.KJV: 3:22 Who is gone into heauen, and is on the right hand of God, Angels, and authorities, and powers being made subiect vnto him.
text.NABRE: 3:22 who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, with angels, authorities, and powers subject to him.

text.NRSV: Since therefore Christ suffered in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same intention (for whoever has suffered in the flesh has finished with sin),
text.Rotherham: Christ, then, having suffered in flesh, do, ye also, with the same purpose, arm yourselves,—because, he that hath suffered in flesh, hath done with sins,—
text.KJV: 4:1 For asmuch then as Christ hath suffered for vs in the flesh, arme your selues likewise with the same minde: for hee that hath suffered in the flesh, hath ceassed from sinne:
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - Christian Restraint. Therefore, since Christ suffered in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same attitude (for whoever suffers in the flesh has broken with sin),

text.NRSV: so as to live for the rest of your earthly life no longer by human desires but by the will of God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that, no longer, in men’s covetings, but in God’s will, ye may live, the still remaining time.
text.KJV: 4:2 That he no longer should liue the rest of his time in the flesh, to the lusts of men, but to the will of God.
text.NABRE: 4:2 so as not to spend what remains of one’s life in the flesh on human desires, but on the will of God.

text.NRSV: You have already spent enough time in doing what the Gentiles like to do, living in licentiousness, passions, drunkenness, revels, carousing, and lawless idolatry.
text.Rotherham: For, sufficient, is the bygone time—to have wrought out, the will of the nations, having gone on in wanton ways, covetings, wine-drinkings, revellings, drinking-bouts, and impious idolatries:—
text.KJV: 4:3 For the time past of our life may suffice vs to haue wrought the will of the Gentiles, when we walked in lasciuiousnes, lusts, excesse of wine, reuellings, banquetings, and abhominable idolatries.
text.NABRE: 4:3 For the time that has passed is sufficient for doing what the Gentiles like to do: living in debauchery, evil desires, drunkenness, orgies, carousing, and wanton idolatry.

text.NRSV: They are surprised that you no longer join them in the same excesses of dissipation, and so they blaspheme.
text.Rotherham: Wherein they are taken by surprise that ye run not with them into the same overflow of riotous excess,—uttering defamation:
text.KJV: 4:4 Wherein they thinke it strange, that you runne not with them to the same excesse of riot, speaking euil of you:
text.NABRE: 4:4 They are surprised that you do not plunge into the same swamp of profligacy, and they vilify you;

text.NRSV: But they will have to give an accounting to the one who stands ready to judge the living and the dead.
text.Rotherham: Who shall render an account unto him who is holding in readiness to judge living and dead;
text.KJV: 4:5 Who shal giue accompt to him that is ready to iudge the quicke & the dead.
text.NABRE: 4:5 but they will give an account to him who stands ready to judge the living and the dead.

text.NRSV: For this is the reason the gospel was proclaimed even to the dead, so that, though they had been judged in the flesh as everyone is judged, they might live in the spirit as God does.
text.Rotherham: For, unto this end, even unto the dead, was the glad-message delivered,—in order that they might be judged, indeed, according to men in flesh, but might live according to God in spirit.
text.KJV: 4:6 For, for this cause was the Gospel preached also to them that are dead, that they might bee iudged according to men in the flesh, but liue according to God in the spirit.
text.NABRE: 4:6 For this is why the gospel was preached even to the dead that, though condemned in the flesh in human estimation, they might live in the spirit in the estimation of God. Christian Charity.

text.NRSV: The end of all things is near; therefore be serious and discipline yourselves for the sake of your prayers.
text.Rotherham: But, the end of all things, hath drawn near:—be of sound mind, therefore, and be sober for prayers;
text.KJV: 4:7 But the ende of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober and watch vnto prayer.
text.NABRE: 4:7 The end of all things is at hand. Therefore, be serious and sober for prayers.

text.NRSV: Above all, maintain constant love for one another, for love covers a multitude of sins.
text.Rotherham: Before all things, keeping, fervent, your love, among yourselves, because, love, covereth a multitude of sins;
text.KJV: 4:8 And aboue all things haue feruent charitie among your selues: for charity shall couer the multitude of sinnes.
text.NABRE: 4:8 Above all, let your love for one another be intense, because love covers a multitude of sins.

text.NRSV: Be hospitable to one another without complaining.
text.Rotherham: Showing hospitality one to another without murmuring,—
text.KJV: 4:9 Use hospitalitie one to another without grudging.
text.NABRE: 4:9 Be hospitable to one another without complaining.

text.NRSV: Like good stewards of the manifold grace of God, serve one another with whatever gift each of you has received.
text.Rotherham: Each one, according as he hath received a gift of favour, unto one another, ministering the same, as careful stewards of the manifold favour of God:
text.KJV: 4:10 As euery man hath receiued the gift, euen so minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.
text.NABRE: 4:10 As each one has received a gift, use it to serve one another as good stewards of God’s varied grace.

text.NRSV: Whoever speaks must do so as one speaking the very words of God; whoever serves must do so with the strength that God supplies, so that God may be glorified in all things through Jesus Christ. To him belong the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: If any one speaketh, as oracles of God, if any one ministereth, as of strength which, God, supplieth,—that, in all things, God may be glorified through Jesus Christ,—unto whom are the glory and the dominion, unto the ages of ages. Amen!
text.KJV: 4:11 If any man speake, let him speake as the oracles of God: if any man minister, let him doe it as of the ability which God giueth, that God in all things may bee glorified through Iesus Christ, to whom be praise and dominion for euer and euer. Amen.
text.NABRE: 4:11 Whoever preaches, let it be with the words of God; whoever serves, let it be with the strength that God supplies, so that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong glory and dominion forever and ever. Amen. IV. Advice to the Persecuted - Trial of Persecution.

text.NRSV: Beloved, do not be surprised at the fiery ordeal that is taking place among you to test you, as though something strange were happening to you.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! be not held in surprise by the burning among you, which, for putting you to the proof, is befalling you, as though a, surprising, thing were happening unto you;
text.KJV: 4:12 Beloued, thinke it not strange concerning the fiery triall, which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened vnto you.
text.NABRE: 4:12 Beloved, do not be surprised that a trial by fire is occurring among you, as if something strange were happening to you.

text.NRSV: But rejoice insofar as you are sharing Christ’s sufferings, so that you may also be glad and shout for joy when his glory is revealed.
text.Rotherham: But, in so far as ye are taking fellowship in the Christ’s sufferings, rejoice! in order that, in the revealing of his glory also, ye may rejoice with exultation:
text.KJV: 4:13 But reioyce in as much as yee are partakers of Christes sufferings; that when his glory shalbe reueiled, ye may be glad also with exceeding ioy.
text.NABRE: 4:13 But rejoice to the extent that you share in the sufferings of Christ, so that when his glory is revealed you may also rejoice exultantly.

text.NRSV: If you are reviled for the name of Christ, you are blessed, because the spirit of glory, which is the Spirit of God, is resting on you.
text.Rotherham: If ye are being reproached in the name of Christ, happy [are ye] ! Because, the Spirit, of the glory, and the [Spirit] of God, unto you is bringing rest.
text.KJV: 4:14 If ye be reproched for the Name of Christ, happie are ye, for the spirit of glory, and of God resteth vpon you: on their part hee is euill spoken of, but on your part he is glorified.
text.NABRE: 4:14 If you are insulted for the name of Christ, blessed are you, for the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you.

text.NRSV: But let none of you suffer as a murderer, a thief, a criminal, or even as a mischief maker.
text.Rotherham: For let, none of you, be suffering as a murderer, or a thief, or an evil-doer, or as one prying into other men’s affairs;
text.KJV: 4:15 But let none of you suffer as a murtherer, or as a theefe, or as an euill doer, or as a busibody in other mens matters.
text.NABRE: 4:15 But let no one among you be made to suffer as a murderer, a thief, an evildoer, or as an intriguer.

text.NRSV: Yet if any of you suffers as a Christian, do not consider it a disgrace, but glorify God because you bear this name.
text.Rotherham: But, if as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but be glorifying God in this name.
text.KJV: 4:16 Yet if any man suffer as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but let him glorifie God on this behalfe.
text.NABRE: 4:16 But whoever is made to suffer as a Christian should not be ashamed but glorify God because of the name.

text.NRSV: For the time has come for judgment to begin with the household of God; if it begins with us, what will be the end for those who do not obey the gospel of God?
text.Rotherham: For it is the ripe time for the judgment to begin with the house of God; but, if first with us, what shall be the end of them who yield not unto the glad-message of God?
text.KJV: 4:17 For the time is come that iudgement must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at vs, what shall the ende bee of them that obey not the Gospel of God?
text.NABRE: 4:17 For it is time for the judgment to begin with the household of God; if it begins with us, how will it end for those who fail to obey the gospel of God?

text.NRSV: And “If it is hard for the righteous to be saved, what will become of the ungodly and the sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And, if, the righteous man, is, with difficulty, saved, where then shall, the ungodly and sinful man, appear?
text.KJV: 4:18 And if the righteous scarcely be saued, where shall the vngodly and the sinner appeare?
text.NABRE: 4:18 “And if the righteous one is barely saved, where will the godless and the sinner appear?”

text.NRSV: Therefore, let those suffering according to God’s will entrust themselves to a faithful Creator, while continuing to do good.
text.Rotherham: So then, let, them who are even suffering, according to the will of God, unto a faithful Creator, be committing their souls, in well-doing.
text.KJV: 4:19 Wherfore, let them that suffer according to the will of God, commit the keeping of their soules to him in well doing, as vnto a faithfull Creator.
text.NABRE: 4:19 As a result, those who suffer in accord with God’s will hand their souls over to a faithful creator as they do good.

text.NRSV: Now as an elder myself and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, as well as one who shares in the glory to be revealed, I exhort the elders among you
text.Rotherham: Elders, therefore, among you, I exhort—[I] who am their co-elder and a witness of the sufferings of the Christ, who also, in the glory about to be revealed, have, a share;—
text.KJV: 5:1 The Elders which are among you I exhort, who am also an Elder, and a witnesse of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partaker of the glory that shall be reuealed.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 - Advice to Presbyters. So I exhort the presbyters among you, as a fellow presbyter and witness to the sufferings of Christ and one who has a share in the glory to be revealed.

text.NRSV: to tend the flock of God that is in your charge, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion but willingly, as God would have you do it—not for sordid gain but eagerly.
text.Rotherham: Shepherd the beloved flock of God, which is among you,—not by compulsion, but by choice, nor yet for base gain, but of a ready mind,—
text.KJV: 5:2 Feede the flocke of God which is among you, taking the ouersight thereof, not by constraint, but willingly: not for filthy lucre, but of a ready minde:
text.NABRE: 5:2 Tend the flock of God in your midst, [overseeing] not by constraint but willingly, as God would have it, not for shameful profit but eagerly.

text.NRSV: Do not lord it over those in your charge, but be examples to the flock.
text.Rotherham: Nor yet as lording it over the allotted portions, but becoming, ensamples, to the beloved flock;
text.KJV: 5:3 Neither as being lords ouer Gods heritage: but being ensamples to the flocke.
text.NABRE: 5:3 Do not lord it over those assigned to you, but be examples to the flock.

text.NRSV: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will win the crown of glory that never fades away.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Chief Shepherd is manifested, ye shall bear away, the unfading crown of glory.
text.KJV: 5:4 And when the chiefe shepheard shall appeare, ye shall receiue a crowne of glory that fadeth not away.
text.NABRE: 5:4 And when the chief Shepherd is revealed, you will receive the unfading crown of glory. Advice to the Community.

text.NRSV: In the same way, you who are younger must accept the authority of the elders. And all of you must clothe yourselves with humility in your dealings with one another, for “God opposes the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”
text.Rotherham: In like manner, ye younger men—submit yourselves unto elders;—All, however, one towards another, gird on humility; because, God, against the haughty, arrayeth himself, whereas, unto the lowly, he giveth favour.
text.KJV: 5:5 Likewise ye yonger, submit your selues vnto the elder: yea, all of you bee subiect one to another, and bee clothed with humilitie: for God resisteth the proud, and giueth grace to the humble.
text.NABRE: 5:5 Likewise, you younger members, be subject to the presbyters. And all of you, clothe yourselves with humility in your dealings with one another, for: “God opposes the proud but bestows favor on the humble.”

text.NRSV: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, so that he may exalt you in due time.
text.Rotherham: Be made lowly, therefore, under the strong hand of God, that he may lift, you, up in due time,—
text.KJV: 5:6 Humble yourselues therefore vnder the mighty hand of God, that hee may exalt you in due time,
text.NABRE: 5:6 So humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time.

text.NRSV: Cast all your anxiety on him, because he cares for you.
text.Rotherham: All your anxiety, casting upon him, because he careth for you.
text.KJV: 5:7 Casting all your care vpon him, for he careth for you.
text.NABRE: 5:7 Cast all your worries upon him because he cares for you.

text.NRSV: Discipline yourselves, keep alert. Like a roaring lion your adversary the devil prowls around, looking for someone to devour.
text.Rotherham: Be sober! be watchful! Your slanderous adversary, as a roaring lion, is walking about—seeking to devour:
text.KJV: 5:8 Be sober, be vigilant: because your aduersary the deuill, as a roaring Lion walketh about, seeking whom he may deuoure.
text.NABRE: 5:8 Be sober and vigilant. Your opponent the devil is prowling around like a roaring lion looking for [someone] to devour.

text.NRSV: Resist him, steadfast in your faith, for you know that your brothers and sisters in all the world are undergoing the same kinds of suffering.
text.Rotherham: Whom resist, steadfast in your faith, knowing that, the same sufferings, in your brotherhood that is in the world, are being accomplished.
text.KJV: 5:9 Whom resist stedfast in the faith, knowing that the same afflictions are accomplished in your brethren that are in the world.
text.NABRE: 5:9 Resist him, steadfast in faith, knowing that your fellow believers throughout the world undergo the same sufferings.

text.NRSV: And after you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, who has called you to his eternal glory in Christ, will himself restore, support, strengthen, and establish you.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the God of all favour—who hath called you unto his age-abiding glory in Christ—when, for a little, ye have suffered, Himself, will adjust, confirm, strengthen:—
text.KJV: 5:10 But the God of all grace who hath called vs into his eternall glory by Christ Iesus, after that ye haue suffered a while, make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you.
text.NABRE: 5:10 The God of all grace who called you to his eternal glory through Christ [Jesus] will himself restore, confirm, strengthen, and establish you after you have suffered a little.

text.NRSV: To him be the power forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Unto him, be the dominion, unto the ages. Amen!
text.KJV: 5:11 To him bee glory and dominion for euer and euer. Amen.
text.NABRE: 5:11 To him be dominion forever. Amen.

text.NRSV: Through Silvanus, whom I consider a faithful brother, I have written this short letter to encourage you and to testify that this is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it.
text.Rotherham: By Silvanus, the faithful brother, as I account him, have I briefly written, unto you, exhorting and adding testimony—that, this, is the true favour of God—within which, stand ye fast!
text.KJV: 5:12 By Syluanus a faithfull brother vnto you, (as I suppose) I haue written briefly, exhorting, & testifying, that this is the true grace of God wherein ye stand.
text.NABRE: 5:12 V. Conclusion - I write you this briefly through Silvanus, whom I consider a faithful brother, exhorting you and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Remain firm in it.

text.NRSV: Your sister church in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings; and so does my son Mark.
text.Rotherham: She who, in Babylon, is co-elect, and Mark my son, salute you:
text.KJV: 5:13 The Church that is at Babylon elected, together with you, saluteth you, and so doth Marcus my sonne.
text.NABRE: 5:13 The chosen one at Babylon sends you greeting, as does Mark, my son.

text.NRSV: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye one another with a kiss of love. Peace unto you all who are in Christ.
text.KJV: 5:14 Greete yee one another with a kisse of charity: Peace bee with you all that are in Christ Iesus. Amen.
text.NABRE: 5:14 Greet one another with a loving kiss. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.
